creator: motoom@xs4all.nl
text:
On Jul 24, 2011, at 00:11 , Glen Barber wrote:

> Perhaps it would be best to reference the archived wavelan site then:
>=20
> http://web.archive.org/web/20040130141721/http://wavelan.com/
>=20
> This takes the user to the main wavelan page, as did the original URL.

Agreed. The original URL wasn't very helpful, either...


>> If the document is still relevant enough, another solution would be
>> to mirror it somewhere where it can be reached with a stable and
>> short URL.
>=20
> I think there are copyright concerns here first, beside "who can host
> the site."

Maybe the copyright concerns could be mitigated by making a redirect =
page? Something like "The document you are looking for can be found on =
http://xyz. You will be redirected there in 5 seconds."

I could put such a page up on =
http://www.michielovertoom.com/freebsd/hcf_wavelan.html

What do you think of that? Or are we spending precious time on a =
non-issue?  Is this document still relevant to FreeBSD? Anyone =
interested could still google for it, if they really want it.

Greetings,


--=20
"Open-source projects are run by people who are self-selected to =
actually
care about the software, as opposed to resentful wage slaves for whom
control over their work product is minimal and development is just =
another
stretch of cubicle time." - ESR, http://esr.ibiblio.org
